context:

---

### GOALS
- **Primary goal(s):** Hypertrophy-focused muscle building — **gain +15 lb lean muscle** and look more athletic while increasing main lifts (timeline **6–8 months**, as of 2026-02-27).
- **Secondary goals:**
  - Get “bigger and stronger” (strength increases as a support outcome to hypertrophy)
  - Maintain consistency with progressive overload and PR-driven training
- **Target events/deadlines:** None specified (time horizon: 6–8 months)

---

#### Schedule & Availability
- **Available training days:** **Mon / Tue / Thu / Fri / Sat**

- Likes/training style: Progressive overload, tracking lifts, hitting PRs
- Focus areas: Hypertrophy + “main lift” strength carryover; overall athletic look

- Main lifts: **Not provided** (as of 2026-02-27)

### LOG
## 2026-02-27 — Profile created/updated (hypertrophy focus)
- Set primary goal: hypertrophy-focused muscle building; aim to add **~15 lb muscle** over **6–8 months**.
- Confirmed training experience: **18 months consistent**, weight increased **145 → 165 lb**, currently eating in a surplus.
- Confirmed schedule: **5x/week** (Mon/Tue/Thu/Fri/Sat), **6 PM**, **~75 min** sessions.
- Confirmed environment: commercial gym with barbells/dumbbells/machines/cables.

**Needed to complete metrics (next update):** current working sets or recent bests for squat/bench/deadlift/OHP, pull-ups/rows, and any body measurements (waist, chest, arms) or estimated body fat %.</Profile>

<Plan>## Header
- **Program name:** Athletic Mass + Strength Carryover (5-Day Hypertrophy Periodization)
- **Program Owner:** GymText
- **User:** Jordan Phillips
- **Goal:** Hypertrophy-focused muscle building (+15 lb lean muscle in ~6–8 months) with steady strength increases on main lifts
- **Duration:** **Ongoing with phase cycling** (12-week cycles)

---

## Program Philosophy
Jordan is an intermediate lifter (18 months consistent) who enjoys progressive overload and PRs, and has the schedule/equipment to support a higher-frequency hypertrophy plan. This program uses a 5-day split that hits each major muscle group **2x/week** (with slightly higher emphasis on upper body for the “athletic look”) while keeping **two “main lift” strength exposures** (upper + lower) to drive measurable performance. Volume is the primary hypertrophy driver (multiple hard sets close to failure), while intensity is sequenced across phases so you can keep adding load without stalling or accumulating excessive fatigue. Sessions fit ~75 minutes by using a consistent exercise menu, clear rest targets, and a blend of compounds + efficient accessories.

**Progression approach:** primarily **double progression** (add reps within a range, then add load), anchored by **RPE targets** and a structured deload every 4th week to keep performance climbing.

---

# Phase 1: Volume Base (Accumulation)
**Length:** 4 weeks (Weeks 1–4)  
**Goal:** Build work capacity, add quality hypertrophy volume, practice stable technique on main lifts.

## Weekly Pattern

### **Mon — Upper Strength (Bench Emphasis)**
- **Focus:** Heavy press + heavy row; moderate accessory volume
- **Volume:** ~16–20 working sets
- **Barbell Bench Press:** 1 × 4–6 @ RPE 8, then 3 × 6–8 @ RPE 7  
  *Main strength driver; back-off sets build pressing volume without frying recovery.*
- **Weighted Pull-Ups (or Neutral-Grip Pulldown):** 4 × 6–8 @ RPE 7–8
- **Incline DB Press:** 3 × 8–12 @ RPE 8
- **Chest-Supported Row (machine or DB):** 3 × 8–12 @ RPE 8
- **Cable Lateral Raise:** 3 × 12–20 @ RPE 8–9
- **Triceps Pressdown (rope):** 2–3 × 10–15 @ RPE 8

---

### **Tue — Lower Strength (Squat Emphasis)**
- **Focus:** Heavy squat pattern + posterior chain; limited junk volume
- **Volume:** ~15–19 working sets
- **Back Squat (high bar or your preferred):** 1 × 4–6 @ RPE 8, then 3 × 6–8 @ RPE 7
- **Romanian Deadlift:** 3 × 6–10 @ RPE 7–8  
  *Hypertrophy + hinge strength carryover without deadlift-level fatigue.*
- **Leg Press:** 3 × 10–15 @ RPE 8
- **Seated or Lying Leg Curl:** 3 × 10–15 @ RPE 8–9
- **Standing Calf Raise:** 3 × 8–12 @ RPE 8
- **Ab Wheel (or Cable Crunch):** 2–3 × 8–12 @ RPE 8

---

### **Thu — Push Hypertrophy (Chest/Delts/Triceps)**
- **Focus:** High-quality pressing volume; shoulder width/shape
- **Volume:** ~18–24 working sets
- **Overhead Press (barbell or DB):** 3 × 6–10 @ RPE 7–8
- **Dumbbell Bench Press:** 3 × 8–12 @ RPE 8
- **Cable Fly (mid or low-to-high):** 3 × 12–20 @ RPE 8–9
- **Lateral Raise (DB or machine):** 4 × 12–25 @ RPE 8–9  
  *High-frequency delts for the “athletic” shoulder cap.*
- **Close-Grip Bench (or Weighted Dips if shoulders tolerate):** 3 × 6–10 @ RPE 8
- **Overhead Cable Triceps Extension:** 2–3 × 10–15 @ RPE 8–9

---

### **Fri — Pull Hypertrophy (Back/Biceps/Rear Delts)**
- **Focus:** Lats + upper back thickness; arm size support
- **Volume:** ~18–24 working sets
- **Chest-Supported T-Bar Row (or machine row):** 4 × 6–10 @ RPE 8
- **Lat Pulldown (wide or neutral):** 3 × 8–12 @ RPE 8
- **1-Arm Cable Row (lat-biased):** 3 × 10–15 @ RPE 8–9
- **Rear Delt Fly (machine or cables):** 3 × 15–25 @ RPE 8–9
- **EZ-Bar Curl:** 3 × 8–12 @ RPE 8
- **Incline DB Curl (or cable curl):** 2–3 × 10–15 @ RPE 8–9

---

### **Sat — Lower Hypertrophy + Arms (Deadlift Technique/Volume)**
- **Focus:** Legs hypertrophy + a second arm touch; hinge without excessive CNS fatigue
- **Volume:** ~18–24 working sets
- **Deadlift (conventional or sumo):** 3 × 4–6 @ RPE 7  
  *Practice + strength exposure, kept submaximal to preserve weekly leg volume.*
- **Bulgarian Split Squat:** 3 × 8–12/side @ RPE 8
- **Leg Extension:** 3 × 12–20 @ RPE 8–9
- **Seated Leg Curl:** 3 × 10–15 @ RPE 8–9
- **Seated Calf Raise:** 3 × 12–20 @ RPE 8–9
- **Cable Curl (or preacher curl):** 2 × 10–15 @ RPE 8–9
- **Rope Pressdown (or machine dip):** 2 × 10–15 @ RPE 8–9

---

## Progression Strategy (Phase 1)
- **Main lifts (bench/squat):**  
  - Keep the **top set** in the prescribed rep range at **RPE ~8**.  
  - When you hit the **top of the rep range** with clean form (e.g., 6 reps @ RPE 8), add **+2.5–5 lb** next week.  
  - Back-off sets: aim to add **1 rep per set** week-to-week until you cap the range, then add load.
- **Deadlift (Sat):** add reps first (toward 6), then add **+5–10 lb** when all sets are within target RPE.
- **Accessories:** double progression within the rep range. When you can hit the **top end on all sets** at target RPE, add the smallest load jump.
- **Deload (Week 4):** reduce **volume ~40–50%** (fewer sets) and keep loads around **RPE 6–7**. Maintain movement patterns; leave the gym feeling better than you arrived.
- **Transition trigger to Phase 2:** after Week 4 deload, if joints feel good and performance is stable/increasing.

---

# Phase 2: Strength-Forward Hypertrophy (Intensification)
**Length:** 4 weeks (Weeks 5–8)  
**Goal:** Heavier loading on main lifts to drive strength PRs while holding enough volume to keep growing.

## Weekly Pattern (same days; adjusted prescriptions)

### **Mon — Upper Strength (Bench Priority)**
- **Barbell Bench Press:** 4 × 3–5 @ RPE 7.5–8.5  
- **Weighted Pull-Ups:** 4 × 5–7 @ RPE 8
- **Incline DB Press:** 2–3 × 8–12 @ RPE 8
- **Chest-Supported Row:** 3 × 8–10 @ RPE 8
- **Lateral Raise:** 3 × 12–20 @ RPE 8–9
- **Triceps (pressdown or skullcrusher):** 2 × 10–15 @ RPE 8–9

### **Tue — Lower Strength (Squat Priority)**
- **Back Squat:** 4 × 3–5 @ RPE 7.5–8.5
- **RDL:** 3 × 6–8 @ RPE 8
- **Leg Press:** 2–3 × 10–15 @ RPE 8
- **Leg Curl:** 3 × 8–12 @ RPE 8–9
- **Calves + Abs:** 4–6 total sets

### **Thu — Push Hypertrophy**
- **Overhead Press:** 4 × 5–8 @ RPE 8
- **DB Bench:** 3 × 8–12 @ RPE 8
- **Cable Fly:** 2–3 × 12–20 @ RPE 9
- **Lateral Raise:** 4 × 12–25 @ RPE 9
- **Triceps (2 movements):** 4–6 total sets @ RPE 8–9

### **Fri — Pull Hypertrophy**
- **Heavy Row (supported):** 4 × 6–8 @ RPE 8
- **Pulldown/Pull-up variation:** 3 × 8–12 @ RPE 8
- **Cable/Machine Row:** 2–3 × 10–15 @ RPE 9
- **Rear delts:** 3 × 15–25 @ RPE 9
- **Biceps (2 movements):** 5–7 total sets @ RPE 8–9

### **Sat — Lower Hypertrophy (Hinge Priority) + Arms**
- **Deadlift:** 4 × 3–5 @ RPE 7.5–8.5
- **Split Squat or Hack Squat:** 3 × 8–12 @ RPE 8
- **Leg Extension:** 3 × 12–20 @ RPE 9
- **Leg Curl:** 2–3 × 10–15 @ RPE 9
- **Optional arms:** 3–4 total sets if time/energy is good

## Progression Strategy (Phase 2)
- **Weeks 5–7:** push load gradually while staying inside RPE targets.  
- **Week 8 deload:** reduce sets by ~40–50%, keep some heaviness (top sets around **RPE 6–7**) to maintain skill.  
- **Transition trigger to Phase 3:** if bar speed is slowing noticeably, sleep/appetite is worse, or pumps are “flat,” you’ll benefit from the specialization phase next.

---

# Phase 3: Hypertrophy Specialization (High-Volume, Targeted)
**Length:** 3 weeks (Weeks 9–11) + 1 week reset/test (Week 12)  
**Goal:** Maximize hypertrophy stimulus (especially delts/upper back/arms) while keeping main lifts “touched” for strength retention.

## Weekly Pattern
- Keep the same split, but:
  - Main lifts become **lower-stress top sets** (1–2 hard sets only).
  - Accessories increase slightly (especially delts/back/arms).

**Main lift prescriptions (all three weeks):**
- **Bench:** 2 × 5–8 @ RPE 7–8 (Mon)
- **Squat:** 2 × 5–8 @ RPE 7–8 (Tue)
- **Deadlift:** 2 × 4–6 @ RPE 7–8 (Sat)

**Accessory emphasis adjustments:**
- Add **+2 sets/week** total to **lateral raises** and **rear delts** (spread across Thu/Fri).
- Add **+2–4 sets/week** total to **biceps + triceps** (spread across Fri/Sat).
- Keep quads/hamstrings at “enough to grow” volume (don’t chase soreness).

### Week 12 — Reset/Test Week (Pick One Option)
- **Option A (recommended for long-term gain):** full deload (same as prior deload rules).  
- **Option B (if feeling great):** “Rep PR week” at **RPE 9 max** (no grinders) on:
  - Bench: best set of 6–10
  - Squat: best set of 6–10
  - Deadlift: best set of 4–8  
  Then deload volume for the remainder of that session.

## Progression Strategy (Phase 3)
- **Accessories:** progress by reps and small load jumps; aim for stable technique and near-failure sets (RPE 8–9) on the final set of each exercise.
- **Fatigue rule:** if performance drops for 2 sessions in a row on the same movement, reduce that movement’s sets by **1–2** for the week.

---

## Phase Cycling
**Order:** Phase 1 (Volume Base) → Phase 2 (Intensification) → Phase 3 (Specialization) → Week 12 Reset → **repeat**.

**What changes each new 12-week cycle:**
- Swap 1–2 accessory variations to prevent staleness (examples: incline DB press ↔ machine press; chest-supported row ↔ cable row; leg press ↔ hack squat).
- Keep the **main lifts consistent** (bench/squat/deadlift/OHP) so you can track true progression and PRs.
- If a body part lags (common: delts/arms/upper back), allocate **+2–4 weekly sets** to it during Phase 3 of the next cycle.

**Transition triggers (autoregulated):**
- If joints feel beat up or motivation tanks early: deload sooner and restart Phase 1.
- If strength is climbing but bodyweight stalls for 2–3 weeks: keep training plan, adjust nutrition (surplus consistency).

---

## Modification History
- **2026-02-27:** Initial program created based on Jordan’s hypertrophy + strength-carryover goal, 5-day availability, and no reported constraints.

--- 

If you share your current working weights (or estimated 1RMs) for bench/squat/deadlift/OHP and your usual rep performance on pull-ups/rows, I can plug in **starting loads** and give you a simple spreadsheet-style week-to-week target table for the first 4 weeks.</Plan>

<Week># Microcycle — Week of 2026-02-23

**Program:** Athletic Mass + Strength Carryover (5-Day Hypertrophy Periodization)  
**Phase:** Phase 1 — Volume Base (Accumulation) (Week 1 of 4)  
**User:** Jordan Phillips  

## Schedule
- **Mon (2/23):** Upper Strength (Bench Emphasis) (commercial gym, 6:00–7:15 PM)
- **Tue (2/24):** Lower Strength (Squat Emphasis) (commercial gym, 6:00–7:15 PM)
- **Wed (2/25):** Off
- **Thu (2/26):** Push Hypertrophy (commercial gym, 6:00–7:15 PM)
- **Fri (2/27):** Pull Hypertrophy (commercial gym, 6:00–7:15 PM)
- **Sat (2/28):** Lower Hypertrophy + Arms (commercial gym, 6:00–7:15 PM)
- **Sun (3/1):** Off

## Week Overview
Week 1 of the Phase 1 accumulation block: establish repeatable working weights that land in the prescribed **RPE ranges** while keeping technique clean. Because no current working weights were provided, the loads below are **conservative starting prescriptions**—use them as your first attempt, then adjust **±5–10 lb** (or the smallest machine pin jump) to hit the target RPE.

---

# Workout — Monday, February 23, 2026
**Focus:** Upper Strength (Bench emphasis: heavy top set + volume back-offs)  
**Location:** Commercial gym  
**Duration:** ~75 minutes  

## Warm-Up (10 minutes)
1. Easy rower or incline walk: **4 minutes**
2. Band shoulder series: **band pull-aparts × 15, face pulls × 15, external rotations × 10/side**
3. Scap push-ups: **× 10**
4. Bench-specific ramp (included in bench warm-up sets below)

## Main Workout

### 1. Barbell Bench Press
**Target:** 1 × 4–6 @ RPE 8, then 3 × 6–8 @ RPE 7  
- **Set 1:** 45 lbs × 10 (warm-up)
- **Set 2:** 95 lbs × 8 (warm-up)
- **Set 3:** 135 lbs × 5 (warm-up)
- **Set 4:** 165 lbs × 3 (warm-up)
- **Set 5:** 185 lbs × 5 (**RPE 8 target**)
- **Back-off Set 1:** 170 lbs × 7 (RPE 7 target)
- **Back-off Set 2:** 170 lbs × 7 (RPE 7 target)
- **Back-off Set 3:** 170 lbs × 7 (RPE 7 target)  
**Rest:** 3–4 minutes between working sets  
**Notes:** Pause the first rep of each working set for ~1 second to standardize touchpoint and tightness. If 185×5 is >RPE 8, drop to **180** for the top set.

### 2. Weighted Pull-Ups (neutral grip if available)
**Target:** 4 × 6–8 @ RPE 7–8  
- **Set 1:** BW + 10 lbs × 7 (RPE 7 target)
- **Set 2:** BW + 10 lbs × 7 (RPE 7–8 target)
- **Set 3:** BW + 10 lbs × 6 (RPE 8 target)
- **Set 4:** BW + 5 lbs × 7 (back-off if grip fades)  
**Rest:** 2–3 minutes between sets  
**Notes:** Full hang → chest to bar path. If pull-ups aren’t there yet, sub **neutral-grip pulldown 140 lbs × 8–10**.

### 3. Incline Dumbbell Press
**Target:** 3 × 8–12 @ RPE 8  
- **Set 1:** 60s × 10 (RPE 7–8)
- **Set 2:** 65s × 10 (RPE 8)
- **Set 3:** 65s × 9 (RPE 8–8.5)  
**Rest:** 2 minutes between sets  
**Notes:** 30–45° bench, slight arch, control the bottom.

### 4. Chest-Supported Row (machine or DB)
**Target:** 3 × 8–12 @ RPE 8  
- **Set 1:** 140 lbs × 10 (RPE 7–8)
- **Set 2:** 150 lbs × 10 (RPE 8)
- **Set 3:** 150 lbs × 9 (RPE 8–8.5)  
**Rest:** 90–120 seconds between sets  
**Notes:** Pull elbows “back and slightly out” to hit upper back; don’t shrug.

### 5. Cable Lateral Raise
**Target:** 3 × 12–20 @ RPE 8–9  
- **Set 1:** 15 lbs × 15 (RPE 8)
- **Set 2:** 15 lbs × 15 (RPE 8–9)
- **Set 3:** 12.5 lbs × 18 (RPE 9; cleaner reps)  
**Rest:** 60 seconds between sets  
**Notes:** Lead with elbow, stop just below shoulder height.

### 6. Rope Triceps Pressdown
**Target:** 3 × 10–15 @ RPE 8  
- **Set 1:** 60 lbs × 12 (RPE 8)
- **Set 2:** 60 lbs × 12 (RPE 8–8.5)
- **Set 3:** 55 lbs × 13 (RPE 8–9)  
**Rest:** 60–75 seconds between sets  
**Notes:** Lock elbows by your ribs; spread rope at the bottom.

## Cool Down (5 minutes)
1. Pec doorway stretch: **30 sec/side**
2. Lat stretch (hanging or bench-assisted): **30 sec/side**
3. Upper back foam roll: **1–2 minutes**

## Notes
- Goal today: leave with a clear idea of your **bench top-set load** for this phase (repeatable, not a grinder).
- Total time: ~75 minutes.

---

# Workout — Tuesday, February 24, 2026
**Focus:** Lower Strength (Squat emphasis + posterior chain)  
**Location:** Commercial gym  
**Duration:** ~75 minutes  

## Warm-Up (10 minutes)
1. Bike: **4 minutes easy**
2. Dynamic hips/ankles: **leg swings × 10/side, ankle rocks × 10/side**
3. Glute activation: **bodyweight hip bridge × 12, bodyweight squat × 8 (slow)**
4. Squat-specific ramp (included below)

## Main Workout

### 1. Back Squat (high bar or preferred)
**Target:** 1 × 4–6 @ RPE 8, then 3 × 6–8 @ RPE 7  
- **Set 1:** 45 lbs × 8 (warm-up)
- **Set 2:** 95 lbs × 5 (warm-up)
- **Set 3:** 135 lbs × 5 (warm-up)
- **Set 4:** 185 lbs × 3 (warm-up)
- **Set 5:** 225 lbs × 2 (warm-up)
- **Set 6:** 255 lbs × 5 (**RPE 8 target**)
- **Back-off Set 1:** 235 lbs × 7 (RPE 7 target)
- **Back-off Set 2:** 235 lbs × 7 (RPE 7 target)
- **Back-off Set 3:** 235 lbs × 7 (RPE 7 target)  
**Rest:** 3–4 minutes between working sets  
**Notes:** Brace hard before descent; keep depth consistent. If 255×5 feels like RPE 9, make top set **245×5–6**.

### 2. Romanian Deadlift (barbell)
**Target:** 3 × 6–10 @ RPE 7–8  
- **Set 1:** 185 lbs × 8 (RPE 7)
- **Set 2:** 205 lbs × 8 (RPE 7–8)
- **Set 3:** 205 lbs × 7 (RPE 8)  
**Rest:** 2–3 minutes between sets  
**Notes:** Soft knees, push hips back, stop when hamstrings limit position (don’t chase the floor).

### 3. Leg Press
**Target:** 3 × 10–15 @ RPE 8  
- **Set 1:** 320 lbs × 12 (RPE 7–8)
- **Set 2:** 360 lbs × 12 (RPE 8)
- **Set 3:** 360 lbs × 11 (RPE 8–8.5)  
**Rest:** 2 minutes between sets  
**Notes:** Controlled depth; don’t bounce out of the bottom.

### 4. Lying Leg Curl
**Target:** 3 × 10–15 @ RPE 8–9  
- **Set 1:** 80 lbs × 12 (RPE 8)
- **Set 2:** 80 lbs × 11 (RPE 8–9)
- **Set 3:** 75 lbs × 13 (RPE 9)  
**Rest:** 75–90 seconds between sets  
**Notes:** Hips pinned to pad; full squeeze at the top.

### 5. Standing Calf Raise
**Target:** 3 × 8–12 @ RPE 8  
- **Set 1:** 140 lbs × 10 (RPE 8)
- **Set 2:** 140 lbs × 10 (RPE 8–8.5)
- **Set 3:** 130 lbs × 11 (RPE 8–9)  
**Rest:** 60–90 seconds between sets  
**Notes:** 1-second pause at the top + 2-second lower.

### 6. Ab Wheel
**Target:** 3 × 8–12 @ RPE 8  
- **Set 1:** × 8
- **Set 2:** × 8
- **Set 3:** × 7 (RPE 8–9)  
**Rest:** 60–90 seconds between sets  
**Notes:** Ribs down; avoid low-back sag.

## Cool Down (5 minutes)
1. Hip flexor stretch: **30 sec/side**
2. Hamstring stretch: **30 sec/side**
3. Deep squat breathing hold: **3 × 20 sec**

## Notes
- Keep squat reps smooth—this block is about repeatable volume more than maxing.
- Total time: ~75 minutes.

---

# Workout — Thursday, February 26, 2026
**Focus:** Push Hypertrophy (chest/delts/triceps volume)  
**Location:** Commercial gym  
**Duration:** ~75 minutes  

## Warm-Up (8–10 minutes)
1. Incline walk or bike: **4 minutes**
2. Shoulder prep: **band dislocates × 10, band face pulls × 15**
3. DB external rotation (light): **2 × 10/side**
4. OHP ramp set(s) included below

## Main Workout

### 1. Overhead Press (barbell)
**Target:** 3 × 6–10 @ RPE 7–8  
- **Set 1:** 45 lbs × 8 (warm-up)
- **Set 2:** 75 lbs × 5 (warm-up)
- **Set 3:** 95 lbs × 3 (warm-up)
- **Set 4:** 110 lbs × 8 (RPE 7–8)
- **Set 5:** 110 lbs × 8 (RPE 8)
- **Set 6:** 105 lbs × 9 (RPE 8; cleaner)  
**Rest:** 2–3 minutes between working sets  
**Notes:** Glutes tight, ribs stacked, head through at the top.

### 2. Dumbbell Bench Press (flat)
**Target:** 3 × 8–12 @ RPE 8  
- **Set 1:** 70s × 10 (RPE 7–8)
- **Set 2:** 75s × 10 (RPE 8)
- **Set 3:** 75s × 9 (RPE 8–8.5)  
**Rest:** 2 minutes between sets  
**Notes:** Slight pause near bottom; keep wrists stacked.

### 3. Cable Fly (mid or low-to-high)
**Target:** 3 × 12–20 @ RPE 8–9  
- **Set 1:** 25 lbs/side × 15 (RPE 8)
- **Set 2:** 25 lbs/side × 15 (RPE 8–9)
- **Set 3:** 20 lbs/side × 18 (RPE 9; better stretch)  
**Rest:** 60–75 seconds between sets  
**Notes:** Let pecs stretch; don’t turn it into a front-delt press.

### 4. Lateral Raise (machine if available; otherwise DB)
**Target:** 4 × 12–25 @ RPE 8–9  
- **Set 1:** 20s DB × 18 (RPE 8)
- **Set 2:** 20s DB × 16 (RPE 8–9)
- **Set 3:** 15s DB × 22 (RPE 9)
- **Set 4:** 15s DB × 20 (RPE 9; short rests)  
**Rest:** 45–60 seconds between sets  
**Notes:** Minimal body English; stop 1–2 reps shy of failure until the last set.

### 5. Close-Grip Bench Press
**Target:** 3 × 6–10 @ RPE 8  
- **Set 1:** 95 lbs × 6 (warm-up)
- **Set 2:** 135 lbs × 8 (RPE 7–8)
- **Set 3:** 145 lbs × 8 (RPE 8)
- **Set 4:** 145 lbs × 7 (RPE 8–8.5)  
**Rest:** 2–3 minutes between working sets  
**Notes:** Hands just inside shoulder width; elbows ~30–45° from torso.

### 6. Overhead Cable Triceps Extension
**Target:** 3 × 10–15 @ RPE 8–9  
- **Set 1:** 45 lbs × 12 (RPE 8)
- **Set 2:** 45 lbs × 11 (RPE 8–9)
- **Set 3:** 40 lbs × 13 (RPE 9)  
**Rest:** 60 seconds between sets  
**Notes:** Big stretch behind head; keep upper arms fixed.

## Cool Down (5 minutes)
1. Triceps stretch overhead: **30 sec/side**
2. Pec minor stretch (corner stretch): **30 sec/side**
3. Light band pull-aparts: **2 × 20**

## Notes
- This is your high-quality “pump” day—control tempo, keep rests honest.
- Total time: ~75 minutes.

---

# Workout — Friday, February 27, 2026
**Focus:** Pull Hypertrophy (back thickness + lats + biceps)  
**Location:** Commercial gym  
**Duration:** ~75 minutes  

## Warm-Up (8–10 minutes)
1. Row erg: **4 minutes easy**
2. Scap + lats: **dead hang 2 × 20 sec**, band pulldowns **× 15**
3. Row-specific ramp set (first set lighter)

## Main Workout

### 1. Chest-Supported T-Bar Row (or supported machine row)
**Target:** 4 × 6–10 @ RPE 8  
- **Set 1:** 135 lbs × 10 (warm-up-ish, RPE 7)
- **Set 2:** 160 lbs × 8 (RPE 8)
- **Set 3:** 160 lbs × 8 (RPE 8–8.5)
- **Set 4:** 150 lbs × 9 (RPE 8; cleaner)  
**Rest:** 2–3 minutes between sets  
**Notes:** Chest glued to pad; pull to lower ribs; 1-second squeeze.

### 2. Lat Pulldown (neutral or wide)
**Target:** 3 × 8–12 @ RPE 8  
- **Set 1:** 130 lbs × 10 (RPE 7–8)
- **Set 2:** 140 lbs × 10 (RPE 8)
- **Set 3:** 140 lbs × 9 (RPE 8–8.5)  
**Rest:** 90–120 seconds between sets  
**Notes:** Drive elbows down; don’t lean back into a row.

### 3. 1-Arm Cable Row (lat-biased)
**Target:** 3 × 10–15/side @ RPE 8–9  
- **Set 1:** 50 lbs × 12/side (RPE 8)
- **Set 2:** 50 lbs × 12/side (RPE 8–9)
- **Set 3:** 45 lbs × 14/side (RPE 9)  
**Rest:** 60–90 seconds between sets (alternate arms)  
**Notes:** Slight torso rotation allowed; think “elbow to back pocket.”

### 4. Rear Delt Fly (machine or cables)
**Target:** 3 × 15–25 @ RPE 8–9  
- **Set 1:** 70 lbs × 20 (RPE 8)
- **Set 2:** 70 lbs × 18 (RPE 8–9)
- **Set 3:** 60 lbs × 22 (RPE 9)  
**Rest:** 60 seconds between sets  
**Notes:** Hands travel wide; keep traps relaxed.

### 5. EZ-Bar Curl
**Target:** 3 × 8–12 @ RPE 8  
- **Set 1:** 60 lbs × 10 (RPE 8)
- **Set 2:** 60 lbs × 10 (RPE 8–8.5)
- **Set 3:** 55 lbs × 11 (RPE 8–9)  
**Rest:** 75–90 seconds between sets  
**Notes:** No hip swing; 2-second lower.

### 6. Incline Dumbbell Curl
**Target:** 3 × 10–15 @ RPE 8–9  
- **Set 1:** 25s × 12 (RPE 8)
- **Set 2:** 25s × 11 (RPE 8–9)
- **Set 3:** 20s × 14 (RPE 9; full ROM)  
**Rest:** 60–75 seconds between sets  
**Notes:** Let arms hang back; get the stretch.

## Cool Down (5 minutes)
1. Lat stretch (bench-assisted): **30 sec/side**
2. Upper trap/neck stretch: **30 sec/side**
3. Thoracic extension on foam roller: **1–2 minutes**

## Notes
- Keep rows strict—back work should feel like back work, not biceps + momentum.
- Total time: ~75 minutes.

---

# Workout — Saturday, February 28, 2026
**Focus:** Lower Hypertrophy + Arms (deadlift technique/volume kept submax)  
**Location:** Commercial gym  
**Duration:** ~75 minutes  

## Warm-Up (10 minutes)
1. Bike: **4 minutes**
2. Hinge patterning: **bodyweight RDL × 10, hip hinge to wall × 8**
3. Hamstring activation: **light leg curl × 15**
4. Deadlift ramp included below

## Main Workout

### 1. Deadlift (conventional or sumo)
**Target:** 3 × 4–6 @ RPE 7  
- **Set 1:** 135 lbs × 5 (warm-up)
- **Set 2:** 185 lbs × 3 (warm-up)
- **Set 3:** 225 lbs × 2 (warm-up)
- **Set 4:** 275 lbs × 1 (warm-up)
- **Set 5:** 315 lbs × 5 (RPE 7 target)
- **Set 6:** 315 lbs × 5 (RPE 7 target)
- **Set 7:** 315 lbs × 4 (RPE 7–7.5 target)  
**Rest:** 3 minutes between working sets  
**Notes:** This should feel like “powerful practice.” If bar speed slows, keep it at **4 reps** rather than grinding to 6.

### 2. Bulgarian Split Squat (DB)
**Target:** 3 × 8–12/side @ RPE 8  
- **Set 1:** 40s × 10/side (RPE 7–8)
- **Set 2:** 45s × 10/side (RPE 8)
- **Set 3:** 45s × 9/side (RPE 8–8.5)  
**Rest:** 90 seconds between sets  
**Notes:** Slight forward torso; knee tracks over toes; control the bottom.

### 3. Leg Extension
**Target:** 3 × 12–20 @ RPE 8–9  
- **Set 1:** 90 lbs × 15 (RPE 8)
- **Set 2:** 90 lbs × 14 (RPE 8–9)
- **Set 3:** 80 lbs × 18 (RPE 9; no swinging)  
**Rest:** 60–75 seconds between sets  
**Notes:** 1-second squeeze at the top.

### 4. Seated Leg Curl
**Target:** 3 × 10–15 @ RPE 8–9  
- **Set 1:** 90 lbs × 12 (RPE 8)
- **Set 2:** 90 lbs × 11 (RPE 8–9)
- **Set 3:** 80 lbs × 14 (RPE 9)  
**Rest:** 60–90 seconds between sets  
**Notes:** Control the eccentric; don’t let the stack slam.

### 5. Seated Calf Raise
**Target:** 3 × 12–20 @ RPE 8–9  
- **Set 1:** 70 lbs × 15 (RPE 8)
- **Set 2:** 70 lbs × 14 (RPE 8–9)
- **Set 3:** 60 lbs × 18 (RPE 9)  
**Rest:** 60 seconds between sets  
**Notes:** Deep stretch at bottom; pause at top.

### 6. Cable Curl
**Target:** 2 × 10–15 @ RPE 8–9  
- **Set 1:** 40 lbs × 12 (RPE 8)
- **Set 2:** 40 lbs × 11 (RPE 8–9)  
**Rest:** 60 seconds between sets  
**Notes:** Elbows slightly forward; constant tension.

### 7. Rope Pressdown
**Target:** 2 × 10–15 @ RPE 8–9  
- **Set 1:** 60 lbs × 12 (RPE 8)
- **Set 2:** 55 lbs × 14 (RPE 9)  
**Rest:** 60 seconds between sets  
**Notes:** Full lockout + rope flare.

## Cool Down (5 minutes)
1. Hamstring stretch: **30 sec/side**
2. Quad stretch: **30 sec/side**
3. Child’s pose breathing: **60 seconds**

## Notes
- Deadlift stays submax by design so your legs can still grow from the rest of the work.
- Total time: ~75 minutes.

---

## Weekly Summary
- **Upper Strength (Mon):** Bench **185×5 top set** + back-offs **170×7×3** (establishing Week 1 baseline for Phase 1 progression).
- **Lower Strength (Tue):** Squat **255×5 top set** + back-offs **235×7×3**; posterior chain supported with RDL **205×8** range.
- **Push Hypertrophy (Thu):** OHP volume around **110×8** plus high-quality delt/triceps work; prioritize clean reps and consistent rests.
- **Pull Hypertrophy (Fri):** Supported row emphasis (strict) + lat volume; biceps finished with controlled eccentrics.
- **Lower + Arms (Sat):** Deadlift practice **315×5×(3 sets)** kept at **RPE ~7** to preserve weekly recovery while still getting hinge exposure.
- **Constraint monitoring:** No current issues—watch shoulder comfort on close-grip bench and elbow tolerance on curls/pressdowns as volume ramps.
- **Next week (Week 2):** If this week lands inside target RPEs, add **+1 rep per back-off set** on bench/squat (or +5 lb if you already cap reps cleanly), and add small jumps on accessories where you hit the top of the rep ranges.

If you send your current best “working sets” (e.g., bench for 6–8 reps, squat for 6–8 reps, deadlift for 4–6 reps, OHP for 6–10), I’ll lock these numbers in so Week 2–4 progressions are dialed rather than estimated.</Week>
